items.title: Bomba says missing hiker Jaslinda fell multiple times during 14-day jungle ordeal
items.summary: TAPAH, June 7 — Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, who was found safe today after going missing for 14 days in the Gunung Batu Putih area here, sustained several minor injuries, including to her head, after falling multiple times while trying to find her way out of the forest. Based on information provided by Jaslinda to rescuers, she fell several times while navigating difficult terrain, including near river areas and from heights of about five and three metres, but managed to continue her journey each time. Earlier, Jaslinda was found by an Orang Asli resident near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, at about 5 pm.

items.title: Jaslinda's rescue an important lesson to hikers to prioritise safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Hikers and outdoor enthusiasts must prioritise safety and proper preparation to prevent dangerous situations in the wilderness, following the recent rescue of a missing hiker in Perak. "Even experienced hikers can encounter difficulties, become disoriented, suffer injuries, or face adverse weather conditions," he said. Hikers must also register with the relevant authorities where required.

items.title: Man arrested for stealing cables on Klang railway tracks
items.summary: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) said it received information at 1.15pm regarding a track circuit disruption at the location. At the time, KTMB Auxiliary Police personnel were conducting a patrol with the D4 Task Force from the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) of the Klang South District Police Headquarters. The suspect was detained following swift action by the KTMB auxiliary police and the South Klang CID team.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia seen retesting 1,700 level amid improved risk sentiment
items.summary: On the index board, the FBM Emas Index increased by 21.94 points to 12,602.06, the FBMT 100 Index gained 24.36 points to 12,441.90, the FBM Emas Shariah Index slipped 33.92 points to 12,570.15, while the FBM Mid 70 Index slipped 184.73 points to 18,299.60, and the FBM ACE Index fell 30.57 points to 4,733.24. Weekly turnover was only 10.73 billion units valued at RM11.32 billion from 14.77 billion units valued at RM21.55 billion a week earlier. The Main Market volume narrowed to 6.10 billion units valued at RM10.31 billion from 9.13 billion units valued at RM20.32 billion previously.

items.title: Johor polls: Rafizi rules out alliances, says Bersama ready to go solo
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 7 — Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) will not enter into negotiations with other political parties and will instead remain an independent contender in the upcoming Johor state election, says Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. The party’s de facto leader said Bersama is steadfast in its decision to contest independently, despite the risk of losing its election deposits. He was accompanied by co-leader Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and other party members.

items.title: Ringgit expected to trade between RM4.02 and RM4.04 against US dollar next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to trade between RM4.02 and RM4.04 against the US dollar next week as investors remain cautious amid prevailing geopolitical uncertainties. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid told Bernama that the stalemate in the United States-Iran negotiations could keep fuel prices elevated, resulting in higher inflationary pressure. "Hence, talks of global interest rate hikes could gather some steam, leading to cautious trading in the currency markets," he told Bernama.

items.title: JKOM chief Ismail Yusop expected to step down today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Community Communications Department (JKOM) director-general Datuk Ismail Yusop is expected to end his service today, after leading the department for more than a year. When contacted yesterday, Ismail directed all inquiries to the Communications Ministry. Check with the Communications Ministry," he said.

items.title: #NSTviral: Tapah Facebook admin stunned and relieved by footage of rescued hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The man behind the Facebook community page Tapah Media Official was left stunned and relieved after receiving images and videos of missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, who was found safe after 14 days in Gunung Batu Putih. Ahmad Muttaqin Khairul, 31, said he received the visuals showing Jaslinda's condition after she was located at Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, at 5.41pm. Earlier, images of Jaslinda at Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, went viral after being shared on the page.

items.title: Teenager found drowned in Kulim mining pool
items.summary: The body of Muhammad Ryan Ziqri Mohd Khairul Nizam was discovered at 3.11am by the Kedah Fire and Rescue Department Water Rescue Team during a surface search. The search and rescue operation was also assisted by the Lunas Voluntary Fire Team and the police. Sofi said the search operation continued last night.

items.title: Missing US student found dead near Kyoto, Japan, mother says
items.summary: NEW YORK: An American student who disappeared while on a family vacation in Japan was found dead outside Kyoto, his mother wrote in a Facebook post on Saturday. The body of James Higginbotham, 20, was discovered in a mountainous area by a volunteer search-and-rescue group, Nancy Higginbotham wrote. The Higginbotham family was visiting Japan to celebrate the high school graduation of James Higginbotham's younger brother.

items.title: South Koreans protest over ballot mishap, demand local election rerun
items.summary: SEOUL: Thousands of South Koreans were demanding a rerun of local elections as they protested into the wee hours of Sunday over ballot paper shortages that disrupted this week's vote. Wednesday's election was the first nationwide vote since President Lee Jae Myung took office following conservative Yoon Suk Yeol's ouster over his brief martial law declaration in late 2024. National Election Commission (NEC) chairperson Rho Tae-ak resigned on Friday after public outrage over ballot paper shortages that the commission said affected 50 polling stations nationwide, including more than 30 in the capital.

items.title: Japan's railways offer lessons beyond transport
items.summary: OSAKA: As Malaysia continues to face recurring train delays and service disruptions, attention often turns to how other countries maintain far greater reliability in their public transport systems. From the Shinkansen to commuter lines, Japan's rail network is defined by strict timetables, discipline and coordination. Punctuality is one of Japan's defining rail characteristics.

items.title: 'Raids alone not enough': Mydin boss wants tougher enforcement against foreign-run businesses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Enforcement against illegal foreign-run businesses must be sustained and not seasonal, says retailer Datuk Dr Ameer Ali Mydin. Ameer, managing director of the Mydin chain of hypermarkets and vice-president of the Malaysia Retailers Association, said retailers had long raised concerns about the growing presence of illegal foreign traders. Anwar raised the issue of illegal foreign-run businesses at Friday's cabinet meeting and instructed authorities to take action against errant traders.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Wild weather swings to be more destructive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians can expect volatile weather conditions as the current dry spell gives way to harsher weather patterns in the coming months, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said. Hisham said Malaysia is currently under the Southwest Monsoon, which is expected to last until mid-September and generally brings drier conditions. However, early-morning squall lines could still trigger sudden heavy rain and strong winds in western Peninsular Malaysia, northern Sarawak and western Sabah, he said.

items.title: 10 killed in Israeli strikes on Gaza
items.summary: GAZA CITY, Palestinian Territories: Gaza's civil defence service reported that Israeli strikes killed 10 people on Saturday, with Israel's military saying one of the dead was a Hamas "cell commander." In Gaza City, a drone strike killed eight people and wounded 15 others in the Jawazat camp for displaced people, according to the civil defence, a rescue service that operates under the authority of Hamas. The city's Al-Shifa Hospital also reported receiving eight bodies.

items.title: Experts: Brace for Super El Nino, extreme weather [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians should brace for prolonged droughts, extreme heat, water shortages, forest fires and severe haze from this month as the country edges towards a "Super El Nino", climatologists said. They said urban flooding is being worsened by overdevelopment and inadequate drainage systems, urging authorities to harness artificial intelligence (AI)-based forecasting and adopt "sponge city" concepts to better absorb heavy rainfall and reduce extreme heat. Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia's Professor Emeritus Dr Fredolin Tangang said the extreme climate phenomenon would affect Malaysia until April or May next year, intensifying the recent swings between heatwaves and flash floods.

items.title: How Malaysians can watch the Fifa World Cup 2026: A complete guide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The Fifa World Cup 2026 will kick off on June 12, but for Malaysians, this edition will be notably different. On May 13, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil confirmed that Malaysians will be able to watch all 104 matches of the 2026 Fifa World Cup through platforms operated by RTM and Unifi TV. The Fifa World Cup 2026 will kick off on June 12.

items.title: Pope calls for end to polarisation on Spain visit
items.summary: MADRID: Pope Leo XIV on Saturday called for an end to "polarising narratives" and "sterile simplifications" on a week-long visit to Spain expected to focus on the divisive issue of immigration. Leo also thanked Spain, whose Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has clashed with US President Donald Trump over Iran and with Israel over the war in Gaza, for its "faithful adherence to international law and multilateralism." Speaking at the royal palace in Madrid, where he was received by King Felipe VI and Queen Letizia, he praised Spain's "active commitment to peace and solidarity among peoples."

items.title: Iran targets Bahrain and Kuwait after new US strikes
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran launched a salvo of missiles at US allies Bahrain and Kuwait on Saturday after fresh American strikes, drawing a furious response from the Gulf monarchies and further straining a fragile truce. US Central Command (CENTCOM) said late Friday its forces shot down four Iranian attack drones launched toward the strait, then struck Iranian coastal surveillance radar sites. CENTCOM said Iran launched seven ballistic missiles towards Bahrain and Kuwait, with six intercepted and one falling short.

items.title: Sustainable jet fuel too costly and scarce, airline group warns
items.summary: RIO DE JANEIRO: Aviation fuel from non-petroleum sources remains difficult to find and still far too expensive to bring about the de-carbonisation of air travel, the world's major airlines said Saturday at a conference in Brazil. Global production of so-called sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) is expected to reach some 2.4 million tons in 2026, only about 0.8 per cent of airline consumption, according to the International Air Transport Association. "It looks to be another disappointing year for SAF production," Willie Walsh, the director general of IATA, which represents about 370 airlines, said in a statement.

items.title: "Albania is not for sale": Albanians step up protests over Trump-linked property development
items.summary: ZVËRNEC, Albanie: Thousands of people rallied Saturday in the Albanian capital and hundreds in a protected nature reserve on the country's coast to protest plans for a luxury beach resort by a company linked to the Trump family. It was the latest in a series of protests against a project whose cost is estimated at around four billion euros (US$4.6 billion), linked to US President Donald Trump's daughter Ivanka Trump and her husband Jared Kushner. Protesters gathered on a sandy beach facing azure waters, some waving red Albanian flags, others carrying inflatable flamingos – the movement's symbol – while chanting: "Cancel the project!"

items.title: Israeli strike kills three Lebanese soldiers
items.summary: BEIRUT: An Israeli strike in southern Lebanon killed three soldiers, Beirut's military said Saturday, as its chief travelled to Pakistan to meet with a key mediator in efforts to end the Middle East war. Israel has launched an operation in Lebanon to root out the Teheran-backed armed group Hizbollah, which drew Lebanon into the wider Middle East war by launching missiles on behalf of its sponsor. UN peacekeepers in Lebanon said "such attacks in Lebanese territory constitute gross violations of Lebanon's sovereignty."

items.title: Mat Sabu urges political stability ahead of Johor election
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu has stressed the need for cooperation between the federal and state governments to ensure political stability and strengthen the country's ability to face future economic challenges. "For the Johor election, we need to ensure there is cooperation between the state and federal governments. Meanwhile, Johor PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan said voters, particularly in Johor, appeared to be becoming more mature in their political choices and were increasingly prioritising stability.

items.title: Anwar: People should not be forced to queue for aid in digital era
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: The Madani government remains committed to ending outdated practices that undermine public dignity, including requiring people to queue for long hours to receive financial aid, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Also present were DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, and PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Also in attendance were PKR vice-president and Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa, Johor Amanah chairman Aminolhuda Hassan and Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching.

items.title: Fire destroys 70-year-old MCA building in Yong Peng
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 70-year-old MCA building in Yong Peng was severely damaged in a fire at Jalan Nipah here tonight. Yong Peng Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Muhammad Amier Syah Hamzah said the department received an emergency call at 8.28pm before deploying a team to the scene. He said firefighters from the Ayer Hitam Fire and Rescue Station also assisted in the operation, which involved two Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) units and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) vehicle.

items.title: NGO calls for election manifestos to be made legally binding documents
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Election manifestos should be made legally binding to ensure political promises are fulfilled once parties are voted into power, says a Johor-based non-governmental organisation. She said manifestos form the basis on which voters decide and grant mandates at federal, state and constituency levels. "As we all know, election manifestos contain promises, plans and proposals offered to voters.

items.title: BN wanted state polls, not Putrajaya, says Anwar
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says state elections were not ideal at a time when the federal government was focused on tackling economic challenges. "The federal government was not ready for an election and wanted to monitor the situation first," he said, adding that Pakatan Harapan (PH) parties were nonetheless prepared for the polls in Johor and Negri Sembilan. "Our focus is on the country and the economy," the PH chairman said at the launch of the Johor PH machinery tonight.

items.title: Bersama to contest Johor polls solo despite risk of losing deposit – Rafizi
items.summary: Its leader Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said although Bersama was only three weeks old, the party was committed to offering voters an alternative choice. He said the party would not hold any negotiations with other political parties and will remain firm in its decision to go it alone. The former economy minister said the party would open applications on Monday for those interested in becoming candidates in the Johor election.

items.title: Aminuddin: Negri Sembilan election triggered by BN reps' withdrawal of support
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Caretaker Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has described the withdrawal of support by Barisan Nasional (BN) representatives from his administration as a betrayal that ultimately led to the Negri Sembilan election. Aminuddin said his government had been functioning well, with development projects progressing and efforts underway to improve people's livelihoods, before political instability emerged. They withdrew their support and the government became unstable," he said.

items.title: Malaysia could gain access to major gas field through Turkmenistan talks – Anwar
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Malaysia has the potential to secure access to a major gas field — described as among the largest in the world — through negotiations between Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas) and Turkmenistan, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. I told Petronas, don't go without me as the trip would take two days," he said. Anwar said despite his tight schedule, including state election commitments, Malaysia's strategic interests in the energy sector required continued engagement to explore global opportunities.

items.title: Man found unconscious at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital, pronounced dead on scene
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Pahang Health Department has confirmed that a man was found unconscious in front of the main lobby of Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) here about 10am today. In a statement, it said a medical team from HTAA's Emergency and Trauma Department was immediately deployed to the scene and confirmed that the man had died. "After initial examination and documentation were completed, the body was sent to the HTAA Forensic Department for further action," it said.

items.title: Encounter with two fishermen leads to safe discovery of Jaslinda
items.summary: Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said Jaslinda was discovered when she approached the fishermen, who were from the same village, while they were at the river. Sabarodzi said preliminary information indicated Jaslinda had been within areas previously covered by search and rescue (SAR) teams, including Gunung Rayu and Bukit Berapit. "She said there were days she survived without proper water, relying on dew before eventually finding a stream," he said.

items.title: Millionaire eatery owner offers recipe for success
items.summary: DAMITH Senanayake came to Kuching from Sri Lanka in his early 20s. Little did his mother know that Damith, as he's commonly known, would still be in Kuching years after graduating. Damith, 34, owns the Ceylonese Restaurant in Kuching.

items.title: PKR annual congress put on hold till Aug 14-16
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 6 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat’s (PKR) Annual Congress has been postponed to Aug 14-16 to allow the party to focus on preparations for the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections. PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the decision was made at the party’s monthly Political Bureau and Central Leadership Council (MPP) meeting held here today. Meanwhile, Fahmi said the meeting also appointed Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and PKR Johor chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa as vice-presidents.

items.title: Hiker missing since May 24 safe, found by local Orang Asli resident in Tapah
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — Hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing while trekking Gunung Batu Putih near Tapah on May 24, was found safe near Kampung Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, this afternoon. Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon said the private company supply chain executive was found by a villager, Nazri Bah Eng, at about 3 pm. Jaslinda was part of a group of 14 hikers and two Forestry Mountain Guides who began the Trans Spencer Chapman expedition at 2 am on May 23 via the Pos Gedung-Gunung Bah Gading-Gunung Batu Putih-Kuala Woh route.

items.title: GIIB founder tells court he faced ‘corporate mafia’ takeover plot and gun threat in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — GIIB Holdings Bhd founder Tai Boon Wee told the High Court that he was targeted in an alleged “corporate mafia” takeover attempt coordinated through a WhatsApp group known as “soldier boys”, and that he was also threatened with a gun at a restaurant, the New Straits Times (NST) reported. He told the court that a group involving Wong and Yee had discussed strategies to take over the listed company through the WhatsApp group. Tai added that the group also allegedly discussed delaying banking processes, manipulating media coverage and issuing negative statements aimed at depressing GIIB’s share price.

items.title: Two investigation papers involving Azam Baki referred to AGC, says Bukit Aman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Two investigation papers involving former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki have been referred to the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) for further action. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar, in a statement today, said that investigations into the cases had been completed and the investigation papers were submitted to the AGC on Thursday (June 4). On Wednesday, Kumar said the police had recorded Azam’s statement under Section 112 of the Criminal Procedure Code to assist in the investigations.

items.title: Man, 42, found dead nearly a week after going missing in Batu Kurau
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — A man with mental disabilities who was reported missing after leaving his home in Simpang 4, Anak Kurau, near Batu Kurau last Sunday was found dead in a plantation area near Teratak Afrina Chalet today. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call on the discovery of the body of Zakaria Shamsudin, 42, at 12.19 pm. On June 1, Zakaria was reported missing after leaving his home at about 4 pm.

items.title: Govt makes recycling facilities compulsory at shopping malls to boost waste management
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — The government has made the provision of Recycling Facilities (FPS) at shopping malls mandatory for the application and renewal of business premises licences under local authorities (PBT), to be implemented in phases starting this month. The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) in a statement today said the matter was agreed upon during the Cabinet meeting yesterday. KPKT said the decision to mandate FPS is a strategic move by the government to strengthen national solid waste management, facilitate public access to recycling facilities, and accelerate the transition to a more sustainable circular economy.

items.title: GTPC launched as national TVET talent platform to bridge industry and graduates, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today officially launched the GITC TVET Placement Centre (GTPC), the nation’s first integrated national talent placement platform aimed at bridging the gap between industry and skilled talent. The platform, launched in conjunction with the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here will serve as a structured ecosystem connecting students, graduates, institutions and industry to facilitate industrial training, apprenticeships and employment opportunities. Ahmad Zahid, who is also the National TVET Council chairman, said National TVET Day now symbolises the central role of TVET in Malaysia’s development agenda.

items.title: PDRM begins early preparations to ensure smooth conduct of Johor state election, says Saifuddin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) have begun initial preparations to ensure that the 16th Johor state election runs smoothly. “Insya-Allah, with careful planning and cooperation of all parties, the Johor state election can be run smoothly, peacefully and in harmony for the people of Johor,” he posted on Facebook today after visiting the Batu Pahat district police headquarters for a briefing on the security forces level of readiness and preparations for the Johor state election. The Election Commission announced that a special meeting for the Johor state election will be held on June 12, following the dissolution of the Johor state assembly on June 1 after the Johor Regent, Tunku Mahkota Ismail gave his consent.

items.title: Ayob Khan: IPCC under Home Ministry gives public independent avenue to report police misconduct
items.summary: KUCHING, June 6 — The Independent Police Conduct Commission (IPCC) provides an alternative platform for the public to lodge complaints related to police misconduct, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said. He said that previously, most complaints involving police misconduct were handled by the Royal Malaysia Police’s (PDRM) Integrity and Standards Compliance Department (JIPS). It aims to enhance integrity within the police force, investigate misconduct involving PDRM personnel and recommend disciplinary action.

items.title: Zahid calls for separate accreditation body to oversee TVET sector
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 (Bernama) -- Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has proposed the establishment of a dedicated accreditation body for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) to coordinate the assessment and recognition of the field without affecting the roles of the Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA) and the Department of Skills Development (JPK). Ahmad Zahid, who is also chairman of the National TVET Council, said the proposal was made in view of the current system, which separates academic and skills-based accreditation, whereas TVET requires a dedicated body to provide more comprehensive coordination. He added that he had discussed the matter with Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman, Higher Education Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Aminuddin Hassim and, prior to that, with Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow: George Town better equipped to preserve Unesco heritage status after 18 years
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 6 — After nearly two decades of being recognised as a Unesco World Heritage Site, Penang is now in a stronger position to manage and preserve George Town due to various efforts undertaken since the recognition on July 7, 2008. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the 18-year journey was not easy as the state government faced various challenges, especially in the first five years after George Town was recognised as a World Heritage Site. He said this at a press conference for the organisation of the George Town Heritage Celebrations (GTHC) 2026 in conjunction with the celebration of George Town’s recognition as a UNESCO World Heritage Site here today.

items.title: Penang Mutiara Line LRT stations taking shape as approval process nears completion, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 6 — Construction of stations for the Mutiara Line Light Rail Transit (LRT) project is progressing steadily, with station designs currently awaiting approval from the Penang Island City Council (MBPP), which is expected soon. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said pier and viaduct works for the Penang International Airport (LTAPP) Station are underway, with five piers already completed. At Bandar Sri Pinang Station, foundation works and station columns have been completed, with the project set to enter its main construction phase soon.

items.title: Titiwangsa’s Tropical Fruit Pavilion opens as centrepiece of KL Architecture Festival 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming today launched the Tropical Fruit Pavilion at Titiwangsa Park, a key attraction of the year-long Kuala Lumpur Architecture Festival 2026 (KLAF 2026). “The pavilion today and also KLAF, it represents a distinctive and thoughtful contribution. Rooted in Malaysia’s tropical heritage and seasonal landscape, the Tropical Fruit Pavilion translates cultural references into spatial installations that explore identity, ecology and public life through architecture.

items.title: Woman with severed leg in Proton X70 crash on LPT2 dies at Dungun Hospital
items.summary: DUNGUN, June 6 — A woman who suffered a severed left leg in an accident at Kilometre 341.5 of the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2) near the Paka Rest and Service Area (R&R) here yesterday died while receiving treatment at Dungun Hospital last night. Dungun Acting Police Chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said police received a report that Maziah Che Hassan, 57, died at 7.15pm. “As a result of the impact, the vehicle spun several times and became lodged on the road barrier,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Housing Ministry revives over 1,500 sick and abandoned projects worth RM148b, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — More than 1,500 sick and abandoned private housing projects nationwide have been successfully revived by the Ministry of Housing and Local Government’s (KPKT) special task force from 2023 to May this year. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the recovery efforts involved 1,576 projects comprising 188,525 housing units, with a total estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion. He said the achievement reflected KPKT’s continued commitment to strengthening the nation’s housing sector through the Task Force on Sick and Abandoned Private Housing Projects (TFST), which was established in 2023.

items.title: No sign of missing climber Jaslinda as Gunung Batu Putih search enters 14th day
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing in the Gunung Batu Putih area, has entered its 14th day without any new leads, although the search has continued on a smaller scale since Thursday. He said that as of Thursday, eight members of the Special, Tactical and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM) and two Forest Mountain Guides (MGP) were still involved in the search sector location, and all of them safely exited at 11.30 am. “Yesterday, there were five search teams consisting of members from the Forestry Department and MGP who entered the search sector location.

items.title: Agong orders swift gazetting of KL green spaces, flood retention ponds, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has decreed that there should be no delay in gazetting Kuala Lumpur’s green spaces and flood retention ponds, Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh said today. “The Yang di-Pertuan Agong decreed that we must not delay the decision on the gazetting of areas such as green zones and flood retention ponds, because these are areas of public interest. Separately, Yeoh said Kuala Lumpur already has sufficient infrastructure and that the current focus is on improving and revitalising public recreational spaces, particularly for families.

items.title: Nga Kor Ming says 4,000 litterbugs now paying fines while sweeping Kuala Lumpur streets for free
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — As of today, over 4,000 litterbugs have been caught so far, said Housing and Local Government (KPKT) minister Nga Kor Ming. Nga said that the Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) now has 4,000 free social workers to help clean up the streets of KL. “KL is going to have not 1,000 but 10,000 facial recognition and high definition CCTV.

items.title: Amir Hamzah assures Sultan Abdullah of consistent federal aid and ECRL boost for Pahang
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 6 — Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan met with the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, last week to provide clarification on federal allocations and assistance channelled to the state. He said the audience enabled him to give a comprehensive briefing covering federal allocations to Pahang, which have shown yearly increases, as well as focusing on priority projects proposed by the state government. “From the briefing, His Royal Highness was able to see that there is indeed a commitment from the federal government to assist states, including Pahang, through the implementation of development projects, as well as allocations for the state, which have been increasing consistently year by year.

items.title: Nga: KL ranked 65th in smart city index as skyscrapers soar and tourists flood in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT) minister Nga Kor Ming revealed that Kuala Lumpur now ranks 65th in the global smart city index. Speaking at the launch of the KL Architecture Festival’s (KLAF) Tropical Fruit Pavilion launch at Titiwangsa Lake Garden this morning, Nga also said that KL is among the top 10 most popular cities in the world in terms of international tourist arrivals according to Mastercard. The launch was also attended by the Federal Territories minister Hannah Yeoh as well as KL mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud.

items.title: MACC launches AI-powered MACCAMPUS platform to boost anti-corruption education in universities
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 6 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today launched MACCAMPUS, a digital platform incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) and gamification to strengthen integrity education and anti-corruption awareness among tertiary students. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the initiative reflects their commitment to engaging university students in enhancing integrity education, corruption prevention and positive values through smartphones and information technology applications. “To ensure MACC remains capable of engaging university students consistently and effectively, it requires a more innovative, interactive and technology-driven educational approach,” he said when speaking at the Integrity Financial Literacy @ Student Anti-Corruption Programme 2026 at Dewan Sultan Mizan, UMT, here.

items.title: Malaysia diversifying energy supply sources to ensure security, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 6 — The government is using a sound risk management approach by diversifying energy supply sources to ensure the country’s energy supply is sufficient, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said the government, through Petroliam Nasional Bhd and oil companies, remains committed to seeking new supply sources, including from South Africa and the United States, to ensure the continuity of economic activities is not affected. “For coal, Malaysia continues to depend largely on Indonesia while expanding its supply sources to other countries, including the United States and Australia.

items.title: Fahmi Fadzil pays tribute to late Pahang PKR leader Abas Awang
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 6 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has conveyed his condolences to the family of Pahang PKR leader Abas Awang, who died yesterday. In a Facebook post today, Fahmi said Abas’s contributions, sacrifices and service to the party and the people will always be remembered. Abas died at his residence in Section 4, Bandar Baru Bangi, Selangor.

items.title: Four suspected cable thieves caught on video along MRR2, police launch manhunt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Police are tracking four men suspected of stealing cables along the side of the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) here after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Ampang Jaya District Police Chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said police detected a viral video at about 5 pm yesterday showing several individuals believed to be stealing cables from a manhole along the highway. Khairul Anuar said the complainant managed to record the incident before lodging the report and the case is being investigated under Section 431A of the Penal Code and Section 235 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.

items.title: MARA TVET institutions record 99.5pc graduate employability rate, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 (Bernama) -- A total of 270 Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) have recorded a graduate employability rate of 99.5 per cent, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. He said the impressive achievement demonstrated that MARA’s TVET institutions are at the forefront of advancing the nation’s TVET agenda, which has now entered the TVET 2.0 phase. “They have embraced TVET 2.0 not only through the various contributions I mentioned earlier, but also by signing agreements with the National TVET Council and most TVET institutions across the country to ensure that all TVET students will be employed by the industry, with 100 per cent absorption into the workforce,” said Ahmad Zahid, who is also chairman of the National TVET Council, in his welcoming remarks at the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here today.

items.title: Anwar: Selected TVET students to receive AI and cryptology training under new govt initiative
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — All government training and research institutions will be required to provide training programmes in emerging fields for selected Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) students beginning July, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said at least 50 to 100 TVET students at each selected institution will be given early training opportunities in emerging and high-technology fields such as artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptology. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, ministers and Shamsul Azri.

items.title: Anwar: Govt approves up to RM50m from PTPK fund to boost TVET 2.0 programme
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — The government has agreed to allow the use of up to RM50 million from the High-Impact Programme fund under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) to strengthen the implementation of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) 2.0, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the use of the fund is subject to conditions, namely that employers and industry players must work closely with the government to ensure the success of the TVET 2.0 agenda. This will also enhance training programmes and exposure opportunities,” he said when officiating the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here today.
